topic: Aetheric Refraction: The Universal Escape Hatch — EXP-539 patch verification (q434)
change_summary: EXP-539 claimed three patches integrated to §7.11 (k=0.097→0.18 fix, dispersion wavelength-dependence caveat, WIN-017/026 shared-property clause). Verification of HEAD (commit 387b28b, sections.json + docs/index.html) shows ALL THREE PATCHES WERE REVERTED by commit fe94fb8 ('decider-2026-06-01: pass-band fix, BAU triage') which pushed a sections.json based on pre-EXP-539 content. ISS-2398, ISS-2400, ISS-2402 are recorded as 'fixed by EXP-539-patch' in closed-issues.json but the prose in HEAD shows none of the patches survived. c1 hole #1 (arcsin 20.6° fix) is independently fixed; c1 holes #2/#3/#4 are still open due to the regression.
our_argument_summary: Argument unchanged from c1 (n(r) at 1.44/2.84/21.53 must produce inseparable optical signatures; absent signatures are absorbed by re-tuning the 0.20 coefficient; that absorbability is the unfalsifiability proof). Patches did not modify the structural argument — they would have tightened the equatorial-coefficient inconsistency, the dispersion-test framing, and the WIN-equivalence claim. None of those edits survived to HEAD.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: No new code analysis since c1. §7.11 critiques a mechanism (n(r)) not a single computed value.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: 0.20 coefficient free; H(r) functional form adjustable; V14 always available — unchanged from c1.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None — section makes no code-level claims.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Falsification-framing section, not tied to a specific monitor.py WIN. WINs referenced (WIN-016 etc.) carry their own monitoring tags. Unchanged from c1.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Aetheric refraction is dome-specific construct; standard atmospheric refraction acknowledged in kernel-of-truth paragraph is a different mechanism. Unchanged.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: The 0.20 coefficient was fitted to V13 data; the absorbability proof shows it is re-tuned whenever new observations disagree. Unchanged.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: n(r) is derived from H(r) — but the derivation is parametric (0.20 is a free fit) not first-principles. Unchanged.

holes_found.description: EXP-539 patches REVERTED by stale-base push (commit fe94fb8). All three patches that the decider commit 8f3655b ('decider: EXP-539 §7.11 patches') integrated into data/sections.json are absent in HEAD (387b28b). Specifically: §1.5 'requires a coefficient of ~0.18' (patch 1 landing in c2 verification) was reverted to 'requires a coefficient of ~0.097'; §7.11 dispersion caveat 'wavelength-dependent n — the dome has not committed' (patch 2) was reverted; §7.11 WIN shared-property clause 'each invoking a free dome-specific parameter with no globe-model counterpart' (patch 3) was reverted. Reproduction: `git show 8f3655b:data/sections.json` vs `git show fe94fb8:data/sections.json` vs HEAD all confirm; `grep -c 'wavelength-dependent n' docs/index.html` returns 0; the user-facing site at funwithscience.net shows the pre-patch prose. ISS-2398, ISS-2400, ISS-2402 are recorded as 'fixed by EXP-539-patch' in monitor/decisions/closed-issues.json (closed_by_run: decider-2026-05-31T21-13), but the closure ledger and the actual file state disagree. Tests passed (3794 passed in fe94fb8 commit message) because no test asserts EXP-539's specific patch strings. This is a pipeline-integrity failure — the decider's clone-and-push discipline allowed an unrelated 'pass-band fix' run to overwrite a recently-landed multi-patch integration.
holes_found.recommendation: Three actions, in order: (1) RE-INTEGRATE EXP-539 patches 2 and 3 verbatim — find_patterns will still match HEAD (verified). (2) AUTHOR a corrected patch 1 — the original find_pattern 'requires a coefficient of ~0.097 — inconsistent with both the current 0.20 and the older ~0.27' targets §1.5 (part1), NOT §7.11 (part7). To close the actual c1 hole #2 about §7.11's '~0.097' claim, an additional patch is needed targeting §7.11 prose 'requires ~0.097;' (semicolon present in the actual phrasing; see find_pattern reproduction below). (3) RE-OPEN ISS-2398, ISS-2400, ISS-2402 (or open new ISS referencing this regression) so decider's next pass treats them as MUST-process. Also surface the regression to tinker as a stale-base-push canary — fe94fb8's diff touched sections.json with content that pre-dated 8f3655b, which suggests the decider in that run did NOT rebase before push, OR rebased successfully but the self-apply patcher worked from a cached pre-rebase blob.

holes_found.description: Cross-section inconsistency in HEAD: §1.5 (part1) and §7.11 (part7) BOTH claim 'n = 1.40 requires ~0.097' — both wrong arithmetically per the c1 verification (k=0.18 is correct at r=10,008 km equatorial radius). The 0.097 figure corresponds to no consistent (n, r) pair the dome publishes. The wrong number appears twice in the user-facing site, with slightly different phrasings ('requires a coefficient of ~0.097' in §1.5, 'requires ~0.097' in §7.11), and a dome defender with a calculator can flag either or both in 30 seconds.
holes_found.recommendation: Both should be corrected to k≈0.18 (or whatever the dome's actual Height Table radius for n=1.40 implies). The patch find_patterns must distinguish the two phrasings — patch 1 in EXP-539 only targets the §1.5 form. A supplemental patch (or a corrected EXP) needs find_pattern 'requires ~0.097' (no 'a coefficient of') for the §7.11 instance.

holes_found.description: Restated carry-over from c1 hole #3 (dispersion wavelength-dependence caveat). The §7.11 'test, executed' paragraph still treats chromatic dispersion as an automatic prediction of n≈2.84 without flagging that constant-n media produce zero dispersion. EXP-539 patch 2 would have closed this hole; patch was reverted. Severity remains minor: the structural argument (angular shift, TIR, ranging) survives regardless.
holes_found.recommendation: Re-apply EXP-539 patch 2 verbatim — find_pattern 'Stars near the southern horizon resolve as sharp points — no rainbow smearing despite a predicted n of 2.84 (for context, diamond at n = 2.42 produces the visible "fire" of cut gemstones).' still matches HEAD.

holes_found.description: Restated carry-over from c1 hole #4 (WIN-017/WIN-026 equivalence shared-property clause). The §7.11 WIN-level impact paragraph still says 'Four further WINs require an equivalent unfalsifiable correction... even though the dome's text frames them differently' WITHOUT naming the shared structural property. EXP-539 patch 3 would have inserted 'each invoking a free dome-specific parameter with no globe-model counterpart' — that addition was reverted. Severity remains minor: the equivalence is structurally correct but reads as asserted-not-shown in current prose.
holes_found.recommendation: Re-apply EXP-539 patch 3 verbatim — find_pattern 'Four further WINs require an equivalent unfalsifiable correction to reconcile dome geometry with observation, even though the dome\'s text frames them differently:' still matches HEAD.

unresolved_prior_cycle.note: Closed in closed-issues.json as 'fixed by EXP-539-patch' but patch was reverted; needs re-open or new ISS

stronger_arguments.description: The cross-section inconsistency could be turned into an argument FOR the dome's piecemeal-table thesis: if our own review can have two different phrasings of the same wrong coefficient living simultaneously in §1.5 and §7.11, the dome's 19–31% discrepancies between its own Height Table values and the V13 formula at the same radii are structurally explicable. We are the wrong place for the argument to live, but the meta-point is worth making in the post-fix prose: the dome's own multi-version-fitting is what produces the 0.097/0.18/0.20/0.27 zoo.

The §7.11 conclusion would be strengthened by citing the regression itself as a real-world demonstration of un-rebased writes overwriting patches — the dome model's V1-V13 'absorbability' is the same pattern at much larger scale. (Note: this is a meta-argument; do NOT actually surface our pipeline mishap on the user-facing site. Mentioning here for completeness of the curmudgeon's adversarial-attack catalog.)

kernel_of_truth.description: The kernel-of-truth paragraph from c1 still holds (atmospheric refraction is real, measurable, ~0.57° at the horizon, Bennett's formula). The EXP-539 patches did not touch that paragraph and the regression did not damage it (data/sections.json:part7 kernel paragraph intact; verified by reading HEAD §7.11 paragraph 5).
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Same as c1 — standard atmospheric refraction is a constraint on observation (measured value, derived mechanism, wavelength-dependent dispersion), aetheric refraction is an accommodation of observation (0.20 coefficient with no first-principles derivation per data/sections.json:§1.5; H(r) adjustable across V1–V13 per data/sections.json:§7.11 absorbability paragraph; predicted optical signatures absent from Gaia/Hipparcos/VLBI/ILRS per data/sections.json:§7.11 paragraph 2). The regression does NOT undermine this distinction — it only leaves the section less polished than it would be after EXP-539.

deeper_analysis:
VERIFICATION RESULT: queue item 434 claimed 'EXP-539 3 patches integrated to §7.11... Verify patches land correctly and argument holds.' Strict reading: the patches did not land in HEAD. Audit trail:

(a) Commit 8f3655b ('decider: EXP-539 §7.11 patches + EXP-468 revision closed') did integrate all three patches — verified by reading 8f3655b:data/sections.json. However, patch 1's find_pattern matched §1.5 (part1, idx 36145) rather than §7.11 (part7, idx 591200), because the EXP-539 find_pattern 'requires a coefficient of ~0.097 — inconsistent with both the current 0.20 and the older ~0.27' matches the §1.5 phrasing but not the §7.11 phrasing 'requires ~0.097;'. So even in 8f3655b's snapshot, the §7.11 instance the c1 hole #2 actually flagged was NOT fixed — only the §1.5 sister-instance was. This is an EXP-authoring bug (find_pattern targeting the wrong section).

(b) Commit fe94fb8 ('decider-2026-06-01: pass-band fix, BAU triage') subsequently pushed a data/sections.json that reverted patches 1, 2, and 3. The commit message and stated scope are §6.14 'Pass band' → 'pass-band' plus BAU triage routing — entirely unrelated to §7.11. Diff inspection (git show fe94fb8 -- data/sections.json) confirms the sections.json content prior to that commit's edits was pre-8f3655b. This is a stale-base regression: the decider's clone (or its self-apply blob cache) was based on a sections.json that predated EXP-539 integration. Two subsequent decider commits (2df48ad, aedee3d) carry the regression forward.

(c) docs/index.html, the build artifact users see at funwithscience.net/dome-model-review/, also lost the patches (grep -c 'wavelength-dependent n' returns 0; grep -c 'invoking a free dome-specific parameter' returns 0; grep -c 'requires a coefficient of ~0.097' returns 1; grep -c 'requires ~0.097' returns 1). So the regression propagated through the build pipeline.

(d) ISS-2398, ISS-2400, ISS-2402 are recorded as 'status: fixed, fixed_by: EXP-539-patch, closed_by_run: decider-2026-05-31T21-13' in monitor/decisions/closed-issues.json. The decider's pop-time issue closure was based on the 8f3655b post-integration commit and did not have any check that subsequent pushes preserve patched content. There is no closure-verification gate.

(e) Tests passed in the regressing commit (3794 passed per fe94fb8 commit message). No test asserts the presence of the EXP-539 patch strings, so the regression slipped through.

Verdict (the section's overall thesis, unfalsifiability of aetheric refraction): unaffected. The c1 verdict 'minor_edit' stands. The section's structural argument does not depend on any of the patched text. But the patched text was supposed to land, and didn't, and the closure ledger says it did — that's a major operational hole.

Advocate mode: a dome defender could quote our §1.5 saying 'requires a coefficient of ~0.097 — inconsistent with both the current 0.20 and the older ~0.27' and our own c1 review (now public via tracker-archive) saying 'k = 0.097 at r = 10,008 km gives n = 1.21, not 1.40' — and conclude that our own arithmetic is internally inconsistent. The defender would be technically right (a curmudgeon flagged it, an EXP attempted to fix it, the fix was reverted, the curmudgeon's evidence and the live prose now disagree). Mitigation: re-apply the patches and add a test that asserts the specific patched strings remain present in sections.json. PROP-051-class hardening.

Cross-WIN consistency: WIN-016, -017, -026, -033, -056, -065, -069 all still consistent with the §7.11 framing. The reverted patch 3 would have tightened the WIN-017/-026 equivalence claim with a structural-property phrase; without it the c1 hole #4 reads as 'asserted not shown' as before. No new cross-WIN drift detected.

Quantitative verification: c1's numerical claims (n(10008)=1.439, n(20015)=2.840, n(40000)=21.528 with k=0.20; arcsin(1/2.84)=20.62°; k=0.18 at r=10,008 km for n=1.40) all still hold. The arcsin 20.6° text in §7.11 is INDEPENDENTLY fixed (c1 hole #1) — verified at part7 idx 36927. So the section is better than c1 in one place (arcsin) and unchanged in three places (the EXP-539-affected lines).

citations_verified: Commit 8f3655b 'decider: EXP-539 §7.11 patches' integrated patches — verified by `git show 8f3655b:data/sections.json` containing 'requires a coefficient of ~0.18', 'wavelength-dependent n', 'invoking a free dome-specific parameter'

Commit fe94fb8 'decider-2026-06-01: pass-band fix, BAU triage' reverted patches — verified by `git show fe94fb8:data/sections.json` containing 'requires a coefficient of ~0.097' and zero matches for the patched strings
HEAD (387b28b) sections.json contains 'requires a coefficient of ~0.097' at part1 idx 31764 and 'requires ~0.097' at part7 idx 38533 — both pre-patch wording
HEAD docs/index.html grep -c 'wavelength-dependent n' returns 0; grep -c 'requires a coefficient of ~0.097' returns 1; build artifact mirrors sections.json regression
ISS-2398, ISS-2400, ISS-2402 all recorded as 'status: fixed, fixed_by: EXP-539-patch' in monitor/decisions/closed-issues.json
§7.11 'arcsin(1/n) ≈ 20.6°' fix (c1 hole #1) is present in HEAD at part7 idx 36927 — independent of EXP-539
citations_failed: EXP-539 commit message claim 'k=0.097→0.18 fix' — verified NOT present in HEAD; the fix is gone
EXP-539 commit message claim 'dispersion wavelength-dependence caveat added' — verified NOT present in HEAD
EXP-539 commit message claim 'WIN-017/026 shared-property clause' — verified NOT present in HEAD
Closure ledger entries for ISS-2398/2400/2402 ('fixed by EXP-539-patch') — disagree with HEAD file state
citations_unchecked: Whether fe94fb8's stale-base push was a clone-without-rebase or a self-apply-with-stale-blob — would need decider-run-trace inspection beyond this review's scope; recommend tinker investigation
Whether any other recent decider commits also silently reverted patches — would need diff scan across recent sections.json edits

advocate_mode.best_defense: A dome defender could argue: 'Your own §1.5 and §7.11 both say n=1.40 requires k≈0.097 and your own c1 curmudgeon review says that figure is mathematically wrong. So one of the two is wrong, by your own admission, and you have not fixed it. Your review is internally inconsistent — by your own quality standard you should withdraw the §1.5/§7.11 absorbability argument until you decide which number you actually mean.' This defense is rhetorically effective because it uses our own apparatus (the curmudgeon review + the closure ledger) as evidence of self-contradiction.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Re-apply the EXP-539 patches AND add a test (test.js or a new test/data/sections-patch-canary.json) that asserts the specific patched strings are present. The test should fail immediately if another stale-base push reverts them. Optionally: surface the regression in a tinker-level operator directive (DIRECTIVE-2026-06-01-stale-base-push-canary) so the pipeline-integrity issue is itself audited.

cross_win_consistency.notes: All seven cited WINs have verdicts consistent with the §7.11 unfalsifiability framing. The reverted patches did not change the WIN list. The c1 cross-WIN observations stand.

claims_checked: patch 1 find_pattern 'requires a coefficient of ~0.097 — inconsistent with both the current 0.20 and the older ~0.27' matches §1.5 (part1) NOT §7.11 (part7); part7 phrasing is 'requires ~0.097;' (semicolon, no 'a coefficient of') — distinct phrasings mean patch 1 would not have targeted §7.11 even without the regression
patch 2 find_pattern matches HEAD §7.11 at part7 dispersion paragraph; replacement strings 'wavelength-dependent n', 'making dispersion a conditional rather than necessary' absent from HEAD
patch 3 find_pattern matches HEAD §7.11 at part7 WIN-impact paragraph; replacement string 'invoking a free dome-specific parameter with no globe-model counterpart' absent from HEAD
Commit 8f3655b 'requires a coefficient of ~0.18' count = 1 (present); fe94fb8 count = 0 (reverted); HEAD count = 0 (still reverted)
quantitative_verification.computations: Same as c1 (H(r) = 8537·exp(-r/8619); n(r,k) = 1 + k·(8537/H(r) − 1)). Additional: byte-level git inspection — `git show 8f3655b:data/sections.json | grep -c 'requires a coefficient of ~0.18'` returns 1; `git show fe94fb8:data/sections.json | grep -c 'requires a coefficient of ~0.18'` returns 0; same query on HEAD returns 0.
quantitative_verification.discrepancies: Three EXP-539 patches missing from HEAD; closure ledger lists ISS-2398/2400/2402 as fixed; reality disagrees.

summary_for_decider:
VERIFICATION FAILURE (q434): EXP-539's three patches did NOT survive to HEAD. Decider commit 8f3655b ('decider: EXP-539 §7.11 patches') integrated them cleanly; decider commit fe94fb8 ('decider-2026-06-01: pass-band fix, BAU triage') reverted ALL THREE via a stale-base push that touched data/sections.json with pre-EXP-539 content. ISS-2398, ISS-2400, ISS-2402 are recorded as 'fixed by EXP-539-patch' in closed-issues.json but the actual file state shows the holes are STILL OPEN. docs/index.html (the user-facing build artifact) also carries the regression. Tests passed in fe94fb8 (3794 passed) because no test asserts the patched strings.

RECOMMENDED ACTIONS (in order):
1. Re-apply EXP-539 patches 2 and 3 verbatim — find_patterns still match HEAD (verified).
2. Patch 1 needs a CORRECTION: its find_pattern 'requires a coefficient of ~0.097 — inconsistent with both the current 0.20 and the older ~0.27' targets §1.5 (part1), NOT §7.11. The c1 hole #2 was specifically about §7.11's 'requires ~0.097' (no 'a coefficient of'). To actually close c1 hole #2, an additional patch is needed for §7.11 with find_pattern 'requires ~0.097;' (or the surrounding sentence). Decider can self-apply both §1.5 (the original patch 1 restored) and §7.11 (new patch).
3. Re-open ISS-2398, ISS-2400, ISS-2402 (or open new ISS-NNNN referencing the regression) so they appear in open-issues.json for the next analyst/decider cycle.
4. Surface to tinker as a stale-base-push canary — operator directive DIRECTIVE-2026-06-01-NNN recommended. fe94fb8's data/sections.json was based on pre-8f3655b content; this indicates either un-rebased clone or self-apply working from a stale blob. Either way, the multi-writer protection (pull --rebase + pre-push integrity gate) failed silently.
5. Add a regression test (test.js or test/data/) that asserts specific patched strings remain present after future commits. Without this, the next stale-base push reverts content silently and no agent notices.

queue_id 434 carried verbatim. Recommended_action: minor_edit (the patched text fixes are small) — but routing should go via decider self-apply of all four patches above, not via a new EXP. PROP-009 queue_id present.

PRELUDE: dome PAT scope verified prior to clone (HTTP 200, prefix github_pat_11AQFYC...).
